excluded: ORM runtime ownership in the root module

goZeroAlignment.strategy: Close sqlx/cache adoption gaps with runnable gofly primitives instead of importing go-zero runtime dependencies into the root module.
requiredEvidence: SQL read/write strategy through SQLStore and NewCluster
transaction examples through SQLStore.Transact and SQL outbox
generated model cache contracts through RedisCachedOrderRepo and UpdateWithInvalidate
Redis/cache observability through cache stats and WritePrometheus
local smoke tests through examples/cache-local and reference-app-smoke
goZeroAlignment.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep existing go-zero sqlx/cache repositories active until storage, cache, generated model, reference app, and framework gap gates pass locally.

statusPolicy.implemented: Capability has repository code, behavior tests, adopter documentation, and a gate.
statusPolicy.planned: Capability is an adoption target and must not be advertised as supported until code, tests, examples, and gates land together.

rows.dependencyBoundary: SQL driver and generated repository dependencies must be owned by root imports or generated project go.mod files, never by unexplained root-module drift.
rows.observabilityEvidence: SQLStore StoreSnapshot records transaction, query, exec, latency, and error counters for adopter troubleshooting.
rows.fallbackBehavior: Fallback to the previous repository transaction path or sqlite-memory smoke fixture when networked SQL dependencies are unavailable.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous storage driver or repository implementation until transaction, generated compile, dependency, and integration gates pass.

rows.dependencyBoundary: Outbox persistence stays in root runtime packages while broker-specific relay dependencies stay in the owning MQ packages or examples.
rows.observabilityEvidence: Outbox enqueue, fetch, delivery, retry, and dead-letter transitions are covered by SQL outbox behavior tests.
rows.fallbackBehavior: Fallback to the prior broker handoff path when outbox relay, delivery, retry, or transaction evidence diverges.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the old event publication route active until SQL outbox smoke, relay, and rollback evidence pass.

rows.dependencyBoundary: Generated Redis cache repositories must keep Redis-only dependencies inside generated projects or examples unless root packages import them directly.
rows.observabilityEvidence: Cache stats and Prometheus output expose hits, misses, loader errors, Redis miss semantics, TTL, and invalidation evidence.
rows.fallbackBehavior: Fallback to GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED=true, direct SQL reads, or local typed cache when Redis is unavailable or invalidation diverges.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable Redis-backed generated cache or pin the previous Redis profile until cache smoke and dependency evidence pass.

rows.dependencyBoundary: Local cache behavior is root-owned and must not introduce generated-project-only dependencies into the root module.
rows.observabilityEvidence: Cache stats, negative-cache protection, bloom-filter protection, disabled mode, and Prometheus output share one troubleshooting path.
rows.fallbackBehavior: Fallback to source-of-truth reads or GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED=true when cache loader, TTL, or protection behavior diverges.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the cache layer for the affected service until local cache stats and generated repository behavior converge.

rows.dependencyBoundary: Generated invalidation helpers must remain deterministic and place generated-only cache dependencies in the generated module.
rows.observabilityEvidence: Generated UpdateWithInvalidate output and RedisCachedOrderRepo evidence make write-through invalidation reviewable before adoption.
rows.fallbackBehavior: Fallback to direct repository writes and explicit cache delete calls when generated invalidation output or compile smoke fails.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep generated cache features behind dry-run and temp-module compile gates until invalidation and dependency placement pass.

generatedEvidence: func (r *OrderRepo) Transact(ctx context.Context, opts *sql.TxOptions, fn func(context.Context, *OrderRepo) error) error
workflowMatrix.observability: SQLStore records transaction timing and failures through StoreStats snapshots.
workflowMatrix.rollbackNote: Keep generated transactional writes behind existing repository transactions until Transact output and SQLStore transaction tests pass.

generatedEvidence: func (r *OrderRepo) ListAfter(ctx context.Context, after int64, limit int) ([]entity.Order, error)
workflowMatrix.observability: SQL pagination paths run through SQLStore query helpers, so query_one/query_all stats remain visible.
workflowMatrix.rollbackNote: Use the previous list endpoint or bounded offset pagination until generated cursor pagination compiles and storage limit tests pass.

generatedEvidence: func (r *%s) UpdateWithVersion(ctx context.Context, in *entity.%s, expectedVersion %s) error
workflowMatrix.observability: Versioned updates use SQLStore exec stats and return storage.ErrNotFound on compare-and-swap misses.
workflowMatrix.rollbackNote: Keep explicit application-level compare-and-swap logic until generated UpdateWithVersion output and tests pass.

workflowMatrix.observability: Generated repository read and write paths route through Cluster-backed SQLStore instances, preserving independent StoreSnapshot database and operation stats.
workflowMatrix.rollbackNote: Use NewOrderRepo with the primary SQLStore until generated cluster-aware repository output, storage routing tests, and replay smoke pass.

workflowMatrix.observability: Cache stats, Redis model cache boundaries, SQL StoreSnapshot, and Prometheus output provide one troubleshooting path for generated services.
workflowMatrix.rollbackNote: Disable Redis-backed cache or set GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED=true when cache stats, Redis miss semantics, or invalidation evidence fail.

workflowMatrix.observability: Generated DB/cache smoke keeps generated-only dependencies inside the temp module and preserves generated-version compatibility evidence.
workflowMatrix.rollbackNote: Keep generated DB/cache features out of adopter templates until temp-module compile and generated-version compatibility gates pass.

p10StorageCacheProductization.goZeroGapClosure: Close sqlx/cache adoption gaps with SQLStore, read/write split, generated transaction helpers, SQL outbox, Redis-backed model cache contracts, cache observability, local smoke, and rollback notes without importing go-zero runtime dependencies.

rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Route all operations to the primary SQLStore or previous repository path until read/write split and transaction tests pass.
Keep existing transactional writes and broker handoff active until generated transactions and SQL outbox smoke pass.
Keep generated cache features behind dry-run and temp-module compile checks until invalidation and dependency placement pass.
Disable Redis-backed cache or use GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED=true when cache stats, miss semantics, TTL, or invalidation evidence diverge.

rows.surface: local smoke and reference app evidence
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Use memory mode as the local fallback and require Docker-backed SQL/Redis evidence before claiming production dependency proof.

p10StorageCacheProductization.promotionPolicy: Treat SQL/cache productization as adopted only when read/write routing, transaction examples, generated model cache contracts, Redis/cache observability, local smoke, generated dependency boundaries, and rollback notes all pass.
p10StorageCacheProductization.runtimeArtifactPolicy: Generated DB/cache smoke projects and Docker-backed reports remain runtime evidence and must stay under ignored temporary paths.

capabilities.adopterValue: Use SQLStore for pooled SQL access, read/write split, sharding helpers, transactions, query stats, dialect placeholders, and identifier validation.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Keep the previous repository implementation pinned until SQLStore tests, generated model compile smoke, and storage integration evidence pass.

capabilities.adopterValue: Use SQL outbox for atomic business writes plus event relay without coupling generated services to a broker-specific transaction model.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Keep the prior broker handoff path active until SQL outbox enqueue, relay, retry, and rollback evidence pass in the reference app.

capabilities.adopterValue: Use typed local cache and optional L1/L2 cache with loader fill, negative-cache protection, bloom filtering, disabled mode, stats, and Prometheus output.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Disable cache with GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED or revert to source-of-truth reads when cache metrics, loader behavior, or invalidation evidence diverge.

capabilities.adopterValue: Use Redis-backed generated model cache through a narrow cache-aside interface with explicit miss semantics and write-through invalidation hooks.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Pin generated repositories to direct SQL reads until Redis miss handling, TTLs, and invalidation are proven for the service.

capabilities.adopterValue: Generate repository-facing SQL or GORM model code while keeping generated-only dependencies in the generated module instead of root go.mod.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Keep generated model output behind dry-run or temp-module compile checks until dependency placement and regeneration diffs are reviewed.

capabilities.adopterValue: Use production-orders and cache-local as runnable evidence for memory fallback, Docker-backed SQL outbox, Redis cache, topology reporting, and rollback notes.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Run memory mode as the default fallback and require Docker mode before claiming production dependency evidence.

promotionCriteria: define migration file layout and rollback semantics
add dry-run diff and checksum evidence
keep generated project dependencies out of root go.mod
capabilities.adopterValue: Future database migration workflow for teams that want generated services and schema evolution evidence in one gate.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Keep using the team's existing migration runner until gofly owns dry-run, rollback, and database integration evidence.

promotionCriteria: add optional Docker-backed Redis cache integration smoke
prove cache miss, hit, TTL, invalidation, and unavailable-Redis fallback
publish CI evidence without requiring local Redis
capabilities.adopterValue: Future Redis-backed cache integration proof beyond fake clients and topology evidence.
capabilities.rollbackNote: Use local typed cache or direct SQL reads until Redis integration evidence exists for the target environment.

p13DBCacheProductization.objective: Productize SQL repository, transaction, pagination, optimistic-lock, read/write split, Redis cache-aside, cache observability, generated DB/cache smoke, and cache hot-path candidate evidence without adding generated-only dependencies to the root module.

rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous hand-written repository path active until generated SQL repository output compiles and storage helper tests pass.

evidence: func (r *OrderRepo) Transact(ctx context.Context, opts *sql.TxOptions, fn func(context.Context, *OrderRepo) error) error
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep business writes on the existing transaction wrapper until generated transaction output and SQLStore rollback tests pass together.

evidence: func (r *OrderRepo) ListAfter(ctx context.Context, after int64, limit int) ([]entity.Order, error)
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Use the previous list endpoint until generated cursor pagination and dialect limit tests remain stable.

evidence: func (r *%s) UpdateWithVersion(ctx context.Context, in *entity.%s, expectedVersion %s) error
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep explicit application-level compare-and-swap logic until generated optimistic-lock output and failure semantics pass.
Use NewOrderRepo with the primary SQLStore until generated cluster-aware repository output, storage routing tests, and replay smoke pass for the service.
Disable Redis-backed generated cache or use direct SQL reads until miss handling, TTL, and invalidation evidence pass.
Keep source-of-truth reads enabled until cache hit, miss, loader error, disabled mode, and Prometheus evidence stay stable.

rows.surface: temporary generated project DB/cache compile smoke
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep DB/cache templates behind dry-run until generated temporary modules compile without root go.mod dependency pollution.

rows.promotionBoundary: P13-05 may move cache hot path from unsupported-report-only to candidate evidence only; P13-06 owns five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation blocking, and latency report-only policy.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep cache performance claims report-only until P13-06 benchmark evidence and bench-regression-check promotion criteria pass.

p13DBCacheProductization.promotionPolicy: DB/cache productization may be advertised only when all implemented rows have source, tests, runtime evidence, generated evidence, gate, rollback notes, and root-module dependency boundaries; cache hot-path performance remains candidate until P13-06 benchmark evidence lands.
p13DBCacheProductization.rootModulePolicy: Generated-project-only SQL, GORM, Redis, and cache dependencies must stay in generated project go.mod files or temporary smoke modules, never as unexplained root go.mod drift.
